case_title: HOPE H. STURGE RN VS. BOARD OF REGEISTERED NURSING,
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER HOPE H. STURGES, R.N.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE MADE A SPECIFIC FINDING OF NO SUBSTANTIAL RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE SINGLE CONVICTION AND THE DUTIES OF A REGISTERED NURSE. THEREFORE, THE DISCIPLINARY ACTION PURSUANT TO B & P 2762 (B) AND (C) ARE INCONSISTENT WITH THE SPECIFIC FINDING OF NO NEXUS AND THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S SPECIFIC FINDING CONTROLS OVER THE IMPLIED NEXUS SET FORTH IN WATSON. PETITIONER'S REQUEST FOR FEES DENIED. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

calendar.matters:
Petitioner Hope H. Sturges, R.N.'S Order To Show Cause (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/15/09)
Off Calendar Feb-04-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Adam Slote of Slote & Links (415.393.8001) for petitioner and moving party
Leslie Brast of the Office of the Attorney General (415.703.5548) for respondent and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER HOPE H. STURGES, R.N.'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE (Per Ex Parte Order Of 12/15/09) is granted. The Administrative Law Judge made a specific finding of no substantial relationship between the single conviction and the duties of a registered nurse. Therefore, the disciplinary action pursuant to B & P 2762 (b) and (c) are inconsistent with the specific finding of no nexus and the Administrative Law Judge's specific finding controls over the implied nexus set forth in Watson. Petitioner's request for fees denied. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Court Reporter: Melanie Gheno CSR #7489
